Stats — The Free Open-Source macOS Menu Bar System Monitor I Use Every Day (CPU, GPU, Temperature, Network)

A walkthrough of Stats — the open-source macOS menu bar system monitor I run on every Mac I own. Covers what it shows (CPU, GPU, RAM, disk, network, battery, sensor temperatures, Bluetooth, multi-timezone clock, plus a Remote module), how to install it with `brew install --cask stats`, a quick tour through each module, the desktop widget trick, and a couple of macOS 26 widget quirks worth knowing about.
Raycast Extensions I Actually Use — Level Up Your Raycast

A friend asked why my project switching and dev workflow feels so snappy on Raycast — turns out it's mostly the extensions. This post is a rundown of the Raycast extensions I actually keep installed: TinyPNG, Kill Process, Visual Studio Code, Google Translate, Speedtest, Format JSON, Base64, and JWT Decoder — with my aliases for each.
Replace Mac's Built-In Spotlight With Raycast

Raycast is a Mac app that can fully replace Spotlight — and it's way more capable. This post walks through installing Raycast, swapping Spotlight's Cmd+Space hotkey over to Raycast, the basic features (open apps, calculator), setting per-app aliases, using Raycast as a Google search bar, the clipboard history feature, and installing extensions like Google Translate from the Raycast Store.
Quickly Clear Old Form Data or Stale Email History from Your Browser

Browsers love to suggest every email and username you (or anyone using your computer) ever typed. This post covers the Shift+Delete trick to remove individual autocomplete entries in Firefox and Chrome without nuking your whole browsing history.
